response.justification: Strong, versatile engineer with 13+ years across web, backend, embedded, and high-throughput distributed systems (Sociomantic/Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/sec), plus relevant low-level C/C++ work and solid software-craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based). However, the role is specifically a Principal/IC6 in growth and experimentation systems, and the resume shows essentially no experience with experimentation platforms, A/B testing, feature flighting, telemetry/metrics infrastructure, or statistical/experimental analysis — the core of this job. There is also limited evidence of principal-level cross-team technical leadership and no Python. The general engineering bar is met, but the domain-specific and seniority signals required for this position are not.
strengths: Distributed/high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec)
Broad multi-language background including C/C++, Java, C#, JS/TS
Strong engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development
concerns: No experimentation/A-B testing, feature-flagging, or rollout-risk experience
No telemetry/metrics infrastructure or statistical/experimental analysis
Limited evidence of principal-level (IC6) cross-team technical leadership
